newsSince: Genuinely new window news: Murata (world's largest MLCC supplier, 40%+ share) will impose comprehensive AI-server and high-end auto MLCC price increases of 10-40% effective July 1; Goldman calls MLCCs the 3rd-largest AI-server cost in a just-beginning supercycle, with a structural deficit widening to 2030 and Murata's new high-end capacity not online before Q4 2026. Stock +13.6% over the window.

sources.label: Murata/Taiyo Yuden MLCC price hikes, GS supercycle (TradingKey)
MLCC manufacturers consider price increase as AI demand outpaces supply (Passive Components)
Murata AI-server power modules mass production 2026 (TrendForce)

verdicts.rankRationale: Passives-alpha lens: the July-1 10-40% AI-server/auto MLCC price hikes plus GS structural-deficit framing are genuinely new and strengthen the pricing/bottleneck case. But on this crowding-sensitive 'rerated benchmark' list at +230% YTD the residual alpha is largely consumed. Small +1, hold rank.
verdicts.bull: 40%+ MLCC share; 10-40% AI-server/auto price hikes from July 1; structural deficit to 2030; AI-server power modules ramping.
verdicts.neutral: Pricing power is now consensus/priced; +230% YTD leaves thin residual edge.
verdicts.bear: Crowded, flow-dominated ownership; +13.6% pop on a name already up 230% YTD invites whipsaw.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: July-1 MLCC price hikes and AI-server power-module/MLCC content growth.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Crowding and AI-capex sensitivity; new capacity arriving late-2026 caps near-term tightness narrative.

verdicts.rankRationale: Master bottleneck lens: the confirmed July-1 10-40% AI-server MLCC price hikes plus GS supercycle/structural-deficit evidence are new fundamental support for the power-delivery/pricing thesis (vs the June 9 hold), and rank 84 understates the scarce pricing-power bottleneck. Up, +2.
verdicts.bull: Dominant MLCC supplier capturing AI-server pricing power with 10-40% hikes into a multi-year structural deficit.
verdicts.neutral: Validated pricing power but mega-cap ($138B) and +230% YTD temper the under-owned-bottleneck score.
verdicts.bear: Crowded mega-cap; late-2026 capacity additions and any AI-capex digestion could deflate ASP gains.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: July-1 MLCC price increases and AI-server MLCC/power-module demand 3.3x by FY30.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Pricing/execution risk and crowded positioning at high YTD.
